revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町]]大竹甲2583番地 |位置 = |祭神 = 少彦名...'
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町]]大竹甲2583番地 |位置 = |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町]]大竹甲2583番地にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。
[[参籠殿]]が[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。
== 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと）
== 歴史 == * [[1934年]]、参籠殿建築される<ref>[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]</ref>。 * [[2013年]]、[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日、本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日、棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ、再発防止策を講じ、修復再開<ref>愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面</ref>。
秋の例大祭までに修復を終わらせる予定。
== 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]]
== 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja}}
* [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁 大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ] 例大祭は3月25日に開かれると書いてあるが、2008年の時点ですでに春の大祭は4月15日、秋の例大祭は10月15日になっている<ref>[http://www.geocities.jp/osukuna/index.htm ｢少彦名神社」と「おすくな社中」物語 ]</ref>。
{{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町]]大竹甲2583番地 |位置 = |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}

秋の例大祭までに修復を終わらせる予定。
== 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]]

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

* [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁 大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ] 例大祭は3月25日に開かれると書いてあるが、2008年の時点ですでに春の大祭は4月15日、秋の例大祭は10月15日になっている<ref>[http://www.geocities.jp/osukuna/index.htm ｢少彦名神社」と「おすくな社中」物語 ]</ref>。

revision.comment: 外部リンクの愛媛神社庁に関する部分を補足訂正しました。外部リンクに「少彦名神社(Facebook)」と「おすくな社中(Facebook)」を加えました。

*愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁 大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ] 当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤りがある（2014年11月現在）ので注意が必要である。 [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁 大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ] 鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[http://www.geocities.jp/osukuna/index.htm ｢少彦名神社」と「おすくな社中」物語 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市]]

revision.comment: 神社の写真を追加しました。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 =[[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|thumb|大洲 少彦名神社 鳥居]]
少彦名神社 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町]]大竹甲2583番地 |ISO = JP-38 |境内1 = 拝殿 |ラベル位置1 = right |緯度度1 = 33|緯度分1 = 30|緯度秒1 = 6.375 |経度度1 =132|経度分1 = 33|経度秒1 = 43.199 |境内2 = 中殿 |ラベル位置2 = right |緯度度2 = 33|緯度分2 = 29|緯度秒2 = 52.22 |経度度2 = 132|経度分2 = 33|経度秒2 = 43.189 |境内3 = 本殿・御陵 |ラベル位置3 = right |緯度度3 = 33|緯度分3 = 29|緯度秒3 = 34.519 |経度度3 = 132|経度分3 = 33|経度秒3 = 46.989 |地図 = Japan Ehime |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町]]大竹甲2583番地にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。 [[参籠殿]]が[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * [[1934年]]、参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]、[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日、本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日、棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ、再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 秋の例大祭までに修復を終わらせる予定。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤り（2014年11月現在）があるので注意が必要である。 [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[http://www.geocities.jp/osukuna/index.htm ｢少彦名神社」と「おすくな社中」物語 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市]]

revision.comment: 拝殿写真、参籠殿写真の追加

少彦名神社 鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2 |ISO = JP-38 |境内1 = 拝殿 |ラベル位置1 = top |緯度度1 = 33|緯度分1 = 30|緯度秒1 = 0.73 |経度度1 =132|経度分1 = 33|経度秒1 = 44.62 |境内2 = 参籠殿 |ラベル位置2 = right |緯度度2 = 33|緯度分2 = 30|緯度秒2 = 2.56 |経度度2 = 132|経度分2 = 33|経度秒2 = 45.23 |境内3 = 中殿 |ラベル位置3 = right |緯度度3 = 33|緯度分3 = 29|緯度秒3 = 52.364 |経度度3 = 132|経度分3 = 33|経度秒3 = 43.059 |境内4 = 本殿・御陵 |ラベル位置4 = bottom |緯度度4 = 33|緯度分4 = 29|緯度秒4 = 34.519 |経度度4 = 132|経度分4 = 33|経度秒4 = 46.989 |地図 = Japan Ehime |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。 参籠殿が[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 参籠殿修復活動が2016年ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の最優秀を受賞した。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * [[1934年]]:参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]:[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日:本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿1棟附棟札及び板図が市指定有形文化財に指定された[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html 大洲市ホームページ（文化スポーツ課・2016年6月2日）]。 * 2016年9月1日:参籠殿修復活動が2016年ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の最優秀を受賞した。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == ギャラリー == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|拝殿・神殿 File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|参籠殿

（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤り（2014年11月現在）があるので注意が必要である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 =[[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
少彦名神社 鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2 |ISO = JP-38 |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |公式HP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ |公式HP名 = 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 |境内1 = 拝殿 |ラベル位置1 = top |緯度度1 = 33|緯度分1 = 30|緯度秒1 = 0.73 |経度度1 =132|経度分1 = 33|経度秒1 = 44.62 |境内2 = 参籠殿 |ラベル位置2 = right |緯度度2 = 33|緯度分2 = 30|緯度秒2 = 2.56 |経度度2 = 132|経度分2 = 33|経度秒2 = 45.23 |境内3 = 中殿 |ラベル位置3 = right |緯度度3 = 33|緯度分3 = 29|緯度秒3 = 52.364 |経度度3 = 132|経度分3 = 33|経度秒3 = 43.059 |境内4 = 御陵 |ラベル位置4 = bottom |緯度度4 = 33|緯度分4 = 29|緯度秒4 = 34.519 |経度度4 = 132|経度分4 = 33|経度秒4 = 46.989 |地図 = Japan Ehime |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。 参籠殿が[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 参籠殿修復活動が2016年ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の最優秀賞を受賞した。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * [[1934年]]:参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]:[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日:本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿1棟附棟札及び板図が市指定有形文化財に指定された[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html 大洲市ホームページ（文化スポーツ課・2016年6月2日）]。 * [[2016年]]9月1日:参籠殿修復活動が2016年ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の最優秀賞を受賞した[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ozu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives 2016 Award of Excellence in 2016]。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == ギャラリー == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|拝殿・神殿 File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|参籠殿
（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤り（2014年11月現在）があるので注意が必要である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.comment: 細かな記述訂正、歴史：1441年と1583年を追加
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 =[[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
少彦名神社 鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2 |ISO = JP-38 |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |公式HP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ |公式HP名 = 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 |境内1 = 拝殿 |ラベル位置1 = top |緯度度1 = 33|緯度分1 = 30|緯度秒1 = 0.73 |経度度1 =132|経度分1 = 33|経度秒1 = 44.62 |境内2 = 参籠殿 |ラベル位置2 = right |緯度度2 = 33|緯度分2 = 30|緯度秒2 = 2.56 |経度度2 = 132|経度分2 = 33|経度秒2 = 45.23 |境内3 = 中殿 |ラベル位置3 = right |緯度度3 = 33|緯度分3 = 29|緯度秒3 = 52.364 |経度度3 = 132|経度分3 = 33|経度秒3 = 43.059 |境内4 = 御陵 |ラベル位置4 = bottom |緯度度4 = 33|緯度分4 = 29|緯度秒4 = 34.519 |経度度4 = 132|経度分4 = 33|経度秒4 = 46.989 |地図 = Japan Ehime |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 =おすくなさん |例祭 =春の大祭：4月15日 秋の例大祭：10月15日 |神事 = }} '''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。 少彦名神社参籠殿は[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 参籠殿修復活動はユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * 1441年:(永享13年/嘉吉1年)宇都宮次郎太郎、少彦名神社へ扁額『少比古廟』を奉納愛媛県史年表 * 1583年:(天正11年)正岡宮内大輔、少彦名神社を再興 * [[1934年]]:参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]:[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日:本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿一棟（附 棟札及び板図）が市指定有形文化財に指定された[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html 大洲市ホームページ（文化スポーツ課・2016年6月2日）]。 * [[2016年]]9月1日:参籠殿修復活動が2016年ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の最優秀賞を受賞した[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ozu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives 2016 Award of Excellence in 2016]。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == ギャラリー == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|拝殿・神殿 File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|参籠殿
（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ|愛媛県史年表=}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤り（2014年11月現在）があるので注意が必要である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形文化財]]

（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ|愛媛県史年表=}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤り（2014年11月現在）があるので注意が必要である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 少彦名神社 |画像 =[[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
少彦名神社 鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2 |ISO = JP-38 |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |公式HP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ |公式HP名 = 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 |境内1 = 拝殿 |ラベル位置1 = top |緯度度1 = 33|緯度分1 = 30|緯度秒1 = 0.73 |経度度1 =132|経度分1 = 33|経度秒1 = 44.62 |境内2 = 参籠殿 |ラベル位置2 = right |緯度度2 = 33|緯度分2 = 30|緯度秒2 = 2.56 |経度度2 = 132|経度分2 = 33|経度秒2 = 45.23 |境内3 = 中殿 |ラベル位置3 = right |緯度度3 = 33|緯度分3 = 29|緯度秒3 = 52.364 |経度度3 = 132|経度分3 = 33|経度秒3 = 43.059 |境内4 = 御陵 |ラベル位置4 = bottom |緯度度4 = 33|緯度分4 = 29|緯度秒4 = 34.519 |経度度4 = 132|経度分4 = 33|経度秒4 = 46.989 |地図 = Japan Ehime |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 =おすくな様、おすくなさん |例祭 =春の大祭：4月15日 秋の例大祭：10月15日 |神事 = }} '''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。 少彦名神社参籠殿は[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 参籠殿修復活動はユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * 1441年:(永享13年/嘉吉1年)宇都宮次郎太郎、少彦名神社へ扁額『少比古廟』を奉納愛媛県史年表 * 1583年:(天正11年)正岡宮内大輔、少彦名神社を再興 * [[1934年]]:参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]:[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリストに登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日:本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿一棟（附 棟札及び板図）が市指定有形文化財に指定された[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html 大洲市ホームページ（文化スポーツ課・2016年6月2日）]。 * [[2016年]]9月1日:参籠殿修復活動がユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ozu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives 2016 Award of Excellence in 2016]。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == ギャラリー == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|拝殿・神殿 File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|参籠殿
（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ|愛媛県史年表=}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。なおリンク先のページにあるマップには誤り（2014年11月現在）があるので注意が必要である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社 ]　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:愛媛県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]]

少彦名神社 鳥居 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2 |ISO = JP-38 |祭神 = [[少彦名命]] |公式HP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ |公式HP名 = 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 |境内1 = 拝殿 |ラベル位置1 = top |緯度度1 = 33|緯度分1 = 30|緯度秒1 = 0.73 |経度度1 =132|経度分1 = 33|経度秒1 = 44.62 |境内2 = 参籠殿 |ラベル位置2 = right |緯度度2 = 33|緯度分2 = 30|緯度秒2 = 2.56 |経度度2 = 132|経度分2 = 33|経度秒2 = 45.23 |境内3 = 中殿 |ラベル位置3 = right |緯度度3 = 33|緯度分3 = 29|緯度秒3 = 52.364 |経度度3 = 132|経度分3 = 33|経度秒3 = 43.059 |境内4 = 御陵 |ラベル位置4 = bottom |緯度度4 = 33|緯度分4 = 29|緯度秒4 = 34.519 |経度度4 = 132|経度分4 = 33|経度秒4 = 46.989 |地図 = Japan Ehime |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 =おすくな様、おすくなさん |例祭 =春の大祭：4月15日 秋の例大祭：10月15日 |神事 = }} '''少彦名神社'''（すくなひこなじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]][[菅田町 (大洲市)|菅田町]]大竹乙937番地2にある[[神社]]。同じ菅田町菅田にも同名の神社が存在する。 少彦名神社参籠殿は[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって{{ill|2014年版「危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリスト」|en|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014_World_Monuments_Watch}}に登録された。 参籠殿修復活動は{{ill|ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞|en|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * 1441年:(永享13年/嘉吉1年)宇都宮次郎太郎、少彦名神社へ扁額『少比古廟』を奉納愛媛県史年表 * 1583年:(天正11年)正岡宮内大輔、少彦名神社を再興 * [[1934年]]:参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]:[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版「危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリスト」に登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日:本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿一棟（附 棟札及び板図）が市指定有形文化財に指定された[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html 大洲市ホームページ（文化スポーツ課・2016年6月2日）]。 * [[2016年]]9月1日:参籠殿修復活動がユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ozu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives 2016 Award of Excellence in 2016]。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == ギャラリー == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|拝殿・神殿 File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|参籠殿
（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ|愛媛県史年表=}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社] 　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社] 　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:愛媛県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]]

[[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿一棟（附 少彦名神社参籠殿は[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって{{ill|2014年版危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリスト|en|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}}に登録された。 参籠殿修復活動は{{ill|ユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞|en|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}。 == 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） == 歴史 == * 1441年:(永享13年/嘉吉1年)宇都宮次郎太郎、少彦名神社へ扁額『少比古廟』を奉納愛媛県史年表 * 1583年:(天正11年)正岡宮内大輔、少彦名神社を再興 * [[1934年]]:参籠殿建築される[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html 議会報告]。 * [[2013年]]:[[ワールド・モニュメント財団]]によって[[2014年]]版「危機に瀕している世界のモニュメントリスト」に登録された。 * [[2014年]]6月4日:本格修復始まる。 * [[2014年]]6月11日:棟梁の男性が転落死し、修復中断。 * [[2014年]]8月1日ごろ:再発防止策を講じ、修復再開愛媛新聞2014年8月2日付6面。 * [[2015年]]3月7日:参籠殿本体の修復工事を終え、竣工式を行う。 * [[2016年]]5月30日:参籠殿一棟（附 棟札及び板図）が市指定有形文化財に指定された[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html 大洲市ホームページ（文化スポーツ課・2016年6月2日）]。 * [[2016年]]9月1日:参籠殿修復活動がユネスコアジア太平洋文化遺産保全賞の2016年最優秀賞を受賞した[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ozu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives 2016 Award of Excellence in 2016]。 == 祭礼 == * 4月15日 - 春の大祭 * 10月15日 - 秋の[[例大祭]] == 施設 == * 大鳥居 * 参籠殿：[[懸造]]を用いて建てられており、床面積の約9割が山崖に迫り出している{{Cite web |title=少彦名神社 {{!}} 大洲市公式観光情報【VisitOzu】愛媛県大洲市 |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=大洲市公式観光情報サイト -Visit Ozu - |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}。 * 拝殿 * 中殿 * 本殿（御陵） == ギャラリー == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|拝殿・神殿 File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|参籠殿
（市指定有形文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ|愛媛県史年表=}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} *愛媛県大洲市には少彦名神社という名称の神社が2つ存在し、愛媛県神社庁にもそれぞれのページがある。 **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社] 　当ページで説明しているのはこちらの神社である。 **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 愛媛県神社庁　大洲市（大洲）少彦名神社] 　鎮座地は大洲市菅田町菅田。 *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ 少彦名神社とおすくな社中 ] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA 少彦名神社（Facebook）] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna おすくな社中（Facebook）] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access 少彦名神社へのアクセス] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなひこなしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:愛媛県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]]

{{Shrine | Name = Sukunahikona Shrine | Image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | Location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|en|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|en|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | Deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|en|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPName = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | Founded = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|en|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|en|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|en|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|en|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|en|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|en|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|en|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Sukunahikona Shrine | Image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | Location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | Deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPName = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | Founded = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] to [[Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]]

Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | Deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecrated = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

{{Shrine | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | Deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. **[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

{{Shrine | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | Deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

{{Shrine | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. {{Shrine | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)-qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)-qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]
