Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|12=draft}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=draft}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft|12=draft}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=draft|12=draft}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|12=draft|12=draft}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=draft|12=draft}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft|12=draft}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|12=draft|12=draft}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=draft|12=draft}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[en:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōzu|ja|大洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|ja|ワールド・モニュメント財団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[en:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]]

Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōzu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:愛媛県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal tangible cultural properties in Ehime]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:愛媛県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}}

The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[sn:Sukunahikona Shrine (Ozu City)]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:大洲市の建築物]] [[Category:大洲市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:愛媛県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}}

Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine.
