{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG|300px]]
Sukunahikona Shrine Torii | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall

Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|qq=|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall

Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Festival: October 15 | Rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Ozu|WD=Q386655}} | coordinates= {{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand [[sn:commons:Category:Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)]][[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17994310]] Wikidata name: Sukunahikona-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Ozu|WD=Q386655}} | coordinates= {{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)]][[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17994310]] Wikidata name: Sukunahikona-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Ozu|WD=Q386655}} | coordinates= {{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)]][[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17994310]] Wikidata name: Sukunahikona-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Ozu|WD=Q386655}} | coordinates= {{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17994310}}

Ehime)]][[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17994310]] Wikidata name: Sukunahikona-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sukunahikona Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sukunahikona torii.JPG | location = [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ōtake Otsu 937-2 | ISO = JP-38 | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} | OfficialHP = https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ | OfficialHPname = Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association | Grounds1 = Worship Hall | LabelPosition1 = top | LatDeg1 = 33 | LatMin1 = 30 | LatSec1 = 0.73 | LonDeg1 = 132 | LonMin1 = 33 | LonSec1 = 44.62 | Grounds2 = Pilgrimage Hall | LabelPosition2 = right | LatDeg2 = 33 | LatMin2 = 30 | LatSec2 = 2.56 | LonDeg2 = 132 | LonMin2 = 33 | LonSec2 = 45.23 | Grounds3 = Middle Hall | LabelPosition3 = right | LatDeg3 = 33 | LatMin3 = 29 | LatSec3 = 52.364 | LonDeg3 = 132 | LonMin3 = 33 | LonSec3 = 43.059 | Grounds4 = Mausoleum | LabelPosition4 = bottom | LatDeg4 = 33 | LatMin4 = 29 | LatSec4 = 34.519 | LonDeg4 = 132 | LonMin4 = 33 | LonSec4 = 46.989 | Map = Japan Ehime | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Alias = Osukuna-sama, Osukuna-san | Festival = Spring Grand Festival: April 15 Autumn Grand Festival: October 15 | Rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Ozu|WD=Q386655}} | coordinates= {{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

coordinates= {{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ōzu, Ehime|Ōzu]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. The restoration activities for the Pilgrimage Hall earned the {{ill|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja|UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Asia Pacific Heritage Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the [[:en:World Monuments Fund|World Monuments Fund]]. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|World Monuments Fund|WD=Q2297150|lt=World Monuments Fund}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (少彦名命) == History == * 1441 (Eikyō 13/Kakitsu 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|World Monuments Fund|WD=Q2297150|lt=World Monuments Fund}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. 1): Utsunomiya Jirōtarō dedicates a plaque titled "Shōhiko Shrine" to Sukunahikona ShrineEhime Prefecture Historical Chronology. * 1583 (Tenshō 11): Masaoka Kōnai Daisuke restores Sukunahikona Shrine. * 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|World Monuments Fund|WD=Q2297150|lt=World Monuments Fund|1=World Monuments Fund}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Buildings in Ozu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

* 1934: Construction of the Pilgrimage Hall[http://muneyasu.net/gikai.html Legislative Report]. * 2013: Registered on the 2014 World Monuments Watch list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|World Monuments Fund|WD=Q2297150|lt=World Monuments Fund|1=World Monuments Fund}}. * June 4, 2014: Full-scale restoration begins. * June 11, 2014: Restoration halts after a lead carpenter falls to his death. * Around August 1, 2014: Restoration resumes with enhanced safety measuresEhime Shimbun, August 2, 2014, p. 6. * March 7, 2015: Restoration of the Pilgrimage Hall is completed, and a Completion tier 6]][[Category: ceremony is held. * May 30, 2016: Pilgrimage Hall (including plaques and diagrams) is designated as an Ōzu City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.ozu.ehime.jp/soshiki/bunkasports/20223.html Ōzu City Website (Cultural and Sports Division, June 2, 2016)]. * September 1, 2016: Restoration activities receive the 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Awards' Award of Excellence[http://www.unescobkk.org/news/article/restoration-of-prayer-hall-at-japan-shrine-wins-top-honor-in-unesco-asia-pacific-heritage-awards/ Restoration of Sanro-Den Hall at Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City, Ehime Prefecture, Japan receives the 2016 Award of Excellence]. == Festivals == * April 15 - Spring Grand Festival * October 15 - Autumn {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} == Facilities == * Grand Torii Gate * Pilgrimage Hall: Built using the {{ill|Kakezukuri|ja|懸造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakezukuri|1=kakezukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94062224}} style, with approximately 90% of the floor area jutting over the mountainside{{Cite web |title=Sukunahikona Shrine {{!}} Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu], Ehime Prefecture |url=https://jp.visitozu.com/archives/highlight/211 |website=Official Ōzu Tourism Information [VisitOzu] |date=2023-03-26 |access-date=2025-01-13 |language=jp}}. * Worship Hall * Middle Hall * Main Hall (Mausoleum) == Gallery == File:Sukunahikona haiden.JPG|Worship Hall and Shrine Hall File:Sukunahikona sanrouden.JPG|Pilgrimage Hall
(Designated Tangible Cultural Property) == References == {{FootnoteHelp|Ehime Prefecture Historical Chronology=}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} * Two shrines named Sukunahikona Shrine exist in Ōzu, Ehime Prefecture. Both have individual pages on the Ehime Shrine Association website: '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=1197 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Ōzu)] - This page describes this specific shrine. '''[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=2807 Ehime Shrine Association – Sukunahikona Shrine in Ōzu City (Sugata)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/ Sukunahikona Shrine and Osukuna Shrine Association] *[https://www.facebook.com/SUKUNAHIKONA Sukunahikona Shrine (Facebook)] *[https://www.facebook.com/osukuna Osukuna Shrine Association (Facebook)] *[https://sites.google.com/site/osukuna/1sukunahikonajinjya/access Directions to Sukunahikona Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|少彦名神社 (大洲市)|version=103318307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:少彦名神社]] [[ja:少彦名神社 (大洲市)]] {{Commons category|Sukunahikona-jinja (Ozu, Ehime)}} {{wikidata link|Q17994310}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ozu City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōzu]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{Coord|33.500203|N|132.562394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sukunahikona Shrine''' (少彦名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at Ōtake Otsu 937-2 in {{ill|Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja|菅田町 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Town (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōzu, Ehime|en|Ōzu, Ehime|WD=Q386655|lt=Ōzu|1=Ōzu, Ehime}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. Another shrine with the same name exists in the Sugata district of the same town. The Pilgrimage Hall of Sukunahikona Shrine was registered on the {{ill|2014 World Monuments Watch|ja|2014_World_Monuments_Watch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2014 World Monuments Watch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} list by the {{ill|World Monuments Fund|en|World Monuments Fund|WD=Q2297150|lt=World Monuments Fund|1=World Monuments Fund}}. Awards|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}' 2016 Award of Excellence{{Cite web |url=https://articles.unesco.org/sites/default/files/medias/fichiers/2023/06/2016-winners_1.pdf |title=Project profiles for 2016 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Awards Winners for Cultural Heritage Conservation |access-date=2025-01-13 |publisher=UNESCO |language=en}}. == deity == * {{ill|Sukunahikona no
